platform.description: Detected platform (e.g., jsPsych, PsyToolkit, lab.js, Gorilla, etc.) or empty if unknown

key.description: Static response key, or null if the key must be resolved dynamically via response_key_js

response_key_js.description: JS expression evaluated on the page that returns the correct key to press. Use when the key-stimulus mapping is randomized per participant (e.g., counterbalanced color-key assignments). The expression should read the current stimulus from the DOM and return the correct key string.

accuracy.description: Per-condition accuracy (0-1). Keys are condition names from stimuli.

omission_rate.description: Per-condition omission rate (0-1). Keys are condition names.

task_specific.description: Task-specific configuration not covered by the standard schema. Two well-known sub-fields the executor reads: key_map (object mapping condition names to key strings or 'dynamic') and trial_timing.max_response_time_ms (integer, deadline in ms if the experiment enforces one). Additional fields may be added freely for task-level documentation.

key_map.description: Maps condition names (matching response.condition in stimuli) to their response key strings. Use 'dynamic' or 'dynamic_mapping' as the value for conditions where the key is resolved at runtime via response_key_js.

trial_timing.description: Trial-level timing parameters read by the executor.

max_response_time_ms.description: Maximum response time deadline enforced by the experiment (ms). When set, the executor caps sampled RTs at this value times rt_cap_fraction.

response_key_js.description: Global JS expression returning the correct response key for the current trial. Evaluated as a fallback when a per-stimulus response_key_js is absent. Useful when a single JS expression resolves the key for all stimuli (e.g., a counterbalanced mapping stored in a global variable).

temporal_effects.description: Temporal effects on the RT series. Each effect is optional — enable only those supported by the literature for this task. Provide rationale citing published studies.

sd_ms.description: Standard deviation of pink noise contribution in ms

hurst.description: Hurst exponent for 1/f noise spectral structure

lag1_pair_modulation.description: Generic 2-back condition-pair RT modulation. Configure one entry per literature-documented (prev_condition, current_condition) transition. The bot's library does not name any specific paradigm effect; supply the condition labels and magnitudes from the literature for THIS paradigm class.

skip_after_error.description: If true, no modulation on the trial following an error (canonical error-contamination guard)

modulation_table.description: List of modulation entries. First entry whose (prev, curr) matches the current trial wins.

delta_ms.description: Fixed RT delta in ms (negative = facilitation, positive = cost). Use this OR delta_ms_min+delta_ms_max.

delta_ms_min.description: Lower bound of uniform-random RT delta in ms

delta_ms_max.description: Upper bound of uniform-random RT delta in ms

post_event_slowing.description: Generic post-event RT slowing. Configure one trigger per literature-documented event type. Available event sources from the runtime: 'error' and 'interrupt'. Use the magnitudes and trigger-priority ordering documented in the literature for THIS paradigm class.

triggers.description: List of triggers in priority order. First matching trigger wins (when exclusive_with_prior_triggers is true, the default).

event.description: Triggering event detected on the previous trial

slowing_ms_min.description: Lower bound of uniform-random RT addition in ms

slowing_ms_max.description: Upper bound of uniform-random RT addition in ms

exclusive_with_prior_triggers.description: If true, this trigger only fires when no earlier trigger in the list matched

between_subject_jitter.description: Between-subject variability parameters. Controls how much each simulated session varies from the base parameters.

rt_mean_sd_ms.description: SD of shared mean RT shift across conditions

sigma_tau_range.description: [min, max] multiplier range for sigma and tau jitter

pilot.description: Pilot run parameters. The executor runs a short pilot session to validate selectors and detection before the full run. Based on the experiment's trial structure (block sizes, condition ratios, practice/test phases), specify enough trials to observe all experimental conditions at least once.

min_trials.description: Minimum trials before pilot can stop

target_conditions.description: Condition labels (matching response.condition in stimuli) that should be observed during pilot

stimulus_container_selector.description: CSS selector for the experiment's main stimulus container element, used for DOM snapshots (e.g., '#jspsych-content')

runtime.description: Runtime configuration for the bot executor. Controls phase detection, timing, advance behavior, and trial interrupt settings.

phase_detection.description: JavaScript expressions evaluated to detect the current task phase.

method.description: Detection method for phase detection expressions. dom_query: uses CSS selector presence/absence (works for jsPsych, lab.js, Gorilla, and most custom HTML tasks). js_eval: evaluates a JS expression directly (required for PsyToolkit and canvas-based tasks where DOM elements do not reflect phase transitions).

complete.description: JS expression returning true when the task is finished

loading.description: JS expression returning true when on a loading/start screen

instructions.description: JS expression returning true when on an instruction page

attention_check.description: JS expression returning true when an attention check is displayed

feedback.description: JS expression returning true when a feedback screen is displayed

practice.description: JS expression returning true during practice trials

test.description: JS expression returning true during test trials (used as fallback)

timing.description: Timing parameters for the executor polling and waiting behavior.

max_no_stimulus_polls.description: Maximum polls with no stimulus before stopping. Canvas-based tasks (PsyToolkit, custom canvas) need ~2000; DOM-based tasks (jsPsych, lab.js, Gorilla, custom HTML) need ~500.

completion_wait_ms.description: Wait duration (ms) after the last trial completes, allowing the experiment to finalize. If the framework's native behavior uploads to a server (e.g., jsPsych + DataPipe), allow ~35000; for frameworks that save locally by default (PsyToolkit default, lab.js typical, most custom HTML), ~5000 is sufficient.

rt_cap_fraction.description: Fraction of max_response_time to cap sampled RTs at

response_window_js.description: JS expression returning true when the experiment's response window is open and its RT timer has started. Use when stimulus detection can fire BEFORE the response window opens (e.g., when a cue is detectable but the response window opens later on the target). Platform examples: PsyToolkit — psy_readkey.keys.includes(KEYCODE) where KEYCODE is the JS keyCode of a response key (e.g., 66 for 'b'); jsPsych — check a jsPsych trial-level JS variable; custom HTML — inspect the source for a JS variable like `window.responseWindowOpen` or a DOM attribute like `data-response-ready='true'` set when the keyboard listener activates. Leave empty if stimulus detection already aligns with the response window.

navigation_delay_ms.description: Milliseconds to pause before pressing the key on a navigation stimulus. Allows the screen to fully render before the keypress is registered. Default 1000 ms.

attention_check_delay_ms.description: Milliseconds to pause before handling an attention check. Simulates a human reading the prompt. Default 1500 ms.

completion_settle_ms.description: Milliseconds to wait after the trial loop ends before attempting data capture. Allows the experiment to finish rendering its completion state. Default 2000 ms.

trial_end_timeout_s.description: Maximum seconds to wait for the response window to close after a trial (i.e., for the next trial's fixation to start). If the window is still open after this timeout the bot proceeds. Default 5.0 s.

trial_context_js.description: JS expression evaluated on the page each trial that returns a string describing the current trial context (e.g., cue identity, block label, or other per-trial metadata). Logged in bot_log.json for debugging and analysis. Leave empty if no per-trial context is needed.

advance_behavior.description: How to advance through instruction/feedback screens between blocks.

pre_keypress_js.description: JS to execute before keypresses (e.g., 'psy_expect_keyboard()' for PsyToolkit)

advance_keys.description: Keys to press to advance between blocks — required when the experiment uses keypress to advance screens.

exit_pager_key.description: Key to exit pager mode (e.g., 'q' for PsyToolkit)

advance_interval_polls.description: Press advance keys every N polls with no stimulus

feedback_selectors.description: CSS selectors for feedback/advance buttons to click

feedback_fallback_keys.description: Keys to press if no feedback button is found — required when the experiment uses keypress to dismiss feedback (otherwise rely on `feedback_selectors`).

trial_interrupt.description: Trial-level interrupt behavior. When detection_condition matches a stimulus condition, the executor polls for that stimulus during the RT wait and probabilistically suppresses the response.

detection_condition.description: Stimulus condition name that triggers mid-trial detection. Empty string for tasks without trial interrupts.

failure_rt_key.description: Distribution key for RTs when suppression fails and a response escapes

failure_rt_cap_fraction.description: Fraction of max_response_time to cap failed-suppression RTs — required when `detection_condition` is set.

inhibit_wait_ms.description: Wait duration (ms) after successful response suppression — required when `detection_condition` is set; a non-zero value is needed or inhibition trials will proceed immediately.

data_capture.description: How to extract the experiment's recorded data after completion.

expression.description: JS expression returning data as string (for js_expression method)

button_selector.description: CSS selector for data button (for button_click method)

result_selector.description: CSS selector for result element (for button_click method)

wait_ms.description: Wait after button click before reading result

attention_check.description: Attention check detection and response configuration.

detection_selector.description: CSS/JS selector to detect when an attention check is displayed

text_selector.description: CSS selector to read attention check prompt text

response_js.description: JS expression returning the correct key to press. This is the primary response mechanism — provide complete parsing logic here.

stimulus_conditions.description: Condition labels (matching response.condition in stimuli) that trigger attention-check handling in the trial loop. Defaults to ["attention_check", "attention_check_response"] when omitted. Override when your stimulus config uses different condition names for attention-check stimuli.

navigation_stimulus_condition.description: Condition label (matching response.condition in stimuli) that marks navigation/advance stimuli handled by pressing their response key. Defaults to "navigation" when omitted. Set this when your stimulus config uses a different condition name for navigation stimuli.
